title: scirpus sylvaticus subsp. radicans
intro: Scirpus sylvaticus subsp. radicans, also known as the woodland club-rush, is a subspecies of perennial aquatic plant that belongs to the sedge family, characterized by its narrow, cylindrical leaves and small, brown flowers. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it a potential treatment for various health issues. The health benefits of Scirpus sylvaticus subsp. radicans include its ability to reduce inflammation, alleviate digestive issues, and support the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
